title: Case No. 0012-2006-PI/TC, Dean of the Lawyers Association of Lima, Constitutional Tribunal, 15 December 2006
text: On 15 December 2006, the Constitutional Tribunal of Peru ruled on whether the Legislative Decree No. 961 (Code of Military and Police Justice) was in accordance with Peruvian Constitution or not. Claims were mainly relating to an alleged violation of Peruvian international obligations relating to IHL. Indeed, according to the claimants, the Code of Military and Police Justice was not in accord with IHL because IHL violations (in particular murder on protected persons) were considered as crimes linked to the duty of the armed forces and therefore subject to military jurisdiction. The Tribunal ruled that such a provision was not in accord to the Peruvian Constitution, because the crime of murder on protected person could not be considered as a crime linked to the military duty. Related laws and/or case-law: Constitution, 1993; Code of Military Justice, 1980; Code of Justice for Military Police, 2006; Code of Military and Police Justice, 2010
original_title: Tribunal Pleno Constitucional, 0012-2006-PI/TC, Sentencia del pleno jurisdiccional del Tribunal Constitucional, Proceso de Inconstitucionalidad, Decana del Colegio de Abogados de Lima
